verbGlossary.CONTRACTS: {source} or developer enters into a prime contract with {target}, establishing a direct contractual relationship with defined scope, compensation, and liability for a project or program.
verbGlossary.SUBCONTRACTS: {source} awards a subcontract to a specialty {target} for a defined scope of trade work, transferring performance obligation while retaining overall project responsibility to the owner.
verbGlossary.PROCURES FROM: Contractor or {target} issues a purchase order or supply agreement to obtain materials, fabricated components, or equipment needed for construction.
verbGlossary.ENGAGES AS SUBCONSULTANT: Lead design professional retains a specialty engineering or consulting {target} under a subconsultant agreement, with the lead firm remaining responsible to the owner for the subconsultant's work.
verbGlossary.COORDINATES WITH: Two {target}s collaborate on overlapping scope, schedule, or spatial conflicts without a direct contractual relationship between them, typically managed through meetings, BIM coordination, or RFIs.
verbGlossary.COMMISSIONS: {source} directly engages a third-{target} firm for independent testing, verification, or investigation services that must remain independent from the design and construction teams.
verbGlossary.SELF-PERFORMS VIA: {target} executes a defined scope of trade work using its own in-house workforce division rather than subcontracting, with costs tracked as direct costs instead of commitments.
verbGlossary.BONDS: Surety {target} issues performance and payment bonds guaranteeing the contractor's contractual obligations, providing financial protection to the owner and downstream subcontractors.
verbGlossary.INSURES: {source} or broker provides coverage policies (general liability, workers' compensation, builders risk, professional liability) that transfer specified construction risks from the insured {target}.
verbGlossary.FINANCES: {source}provides construction lending or project financing, funding the project through periodic draws tied to verified progress milestones.
verbGlossary.OVERSEES: Management {target} monitors and directs the work of another organization on behalf of the owner, providing schedule, budget, quality, and safety oversight without holding direct trade contracts.
verbGlossary.INSPECTS: Regulatory {target} conducts field inspections to verify code compliance, issues citations for violations, and must approve work before it can be concealed or the project can proceed.
verbGlossary.REVIEWS DESIGN OF: Regulatory {target} examines construction documents submitted by the design team for code compliance, issuing plan review comments that must be resolved before permits are granted.
verbGlossary.GRANTS ENTITLEMENT TO: Land use {target} approves a project's zoning compliance, variances, or site plan, which is a legal prerequisite before building permits can be issued.
verbGlossary.PERMITS: {source}issues permits governing construction activities such as stormwater discharge, air quality, or hazardous material handling, with ongoing compliance monitoring.
verbGlossary.VERIFIES WORK OF: {source}inspection agency performs code-required special inspections on the contractor's installed work, con{target}ing structural connections, material placement, or fireproofing meet specifications.
verbGlossary.TESTS MATERIALS FOR: Testing laboratory performs standardized tests on construction materials (concrete, soil, steel, asphalt) to con{target} they meet specification requirements before or after placement.
verbGlossary.VALIDATES SYSTEMS OF: {source}tests and verifies that installed building systems perform according to the design {target} specifications, documenting deficiencies that require correction.
verbGlossary.PROVIDES SERVICE TO: Utility {target} designs, reviews, and connects permanent utility infrastructure to the project, coordinating service capacity, meter placement, and energization with the contractor.
verbGlossary.ADVISES: Specialty {target} provides expert guidance to the design team on code interpretation, sustainability criteria, subsurface conditions, or other technical matters that influence design decisions.
verbGlossary.MEASURES: {source} tracks a defined performance metric that quantifies operational, safety, or commercial outcomes, enabling benchmarking and trend analysis.
